steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's goal is to provide an explanation and example of Kepler's first law. A strong answer must (1) state what the law says and (2) give a concrete, accurate example that illustrates it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Kepler's first law...states that the object being orbited is one of the ellipse's foci': gives only a partial statement of the law and provides no example. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Europa completes an orbit in 3.55 Earth days...a clear example of Kepler's first law': the orbital period is irrelevant to Kepler's first law (which concerns shape, not time); the example does not actually illustrate the law. ✗
  • Choice C — 'planetary satellites orbit their planets in an elliptical fashion': the 'example' is vague and generic rather than a specific, concrete case. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Europa's orbit of Jupiter is elliptical, demonstrating Kepler's first law...applies to moons as well': opens with a specific example (Europa's elliptical orbit), names the law, and correctly notes it extends to moons. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
